checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any fabricated paragraphs or sentences. All headings and body text that are present correspond to content found in the source document. AI-generated metadata like the title and alt-text/ARIA labels are allowed per the instructions.
There is no contact information (phone numbers, emails, URLs) in the source document, and none has been fabricated in the output.

While significant content is missing from within sections 6 and 8, the headings for these sections and their sub-sections are present in the output. No entire page was dropped, as the content before and after the missing text blocks is represented.
The source document does not contain any tables.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output is missing a substantial amount of body text from several sections. Specifically, all the descriptive paragraphs for sub-sections 6.a, 6.b, 6.c, 6.d, 6.e, 8.a, and 8.b are absent. This represents roughly 50% of the total text content of the source document. For example, in section 6.a 'Local Government Committee', the source contains two paragraphs of status information that are completely missing in the output. Similarly, the paragraph under 8.a 'LiDAR Data' is missing.
checks.b_evidence_source: Section 6.a: 'The LGC last met on November 20th... NextGen 911 project.' Section 6.b: 'The executive committee meeting... GICC annual report.' Section 6.c: 'There are two new members... white paper.' Section 6.d: 'Paul mentioned that... May 2020 meeting.' Section 6.e: 'Dan is stepping down... incomplete decisions.' Section 8.a: 'Hope reported that Esri... NC imagery.' Section 8.b: 'March 16, June 22, October 5, December 14. All meetings start at 1pm.'
checks.b_evidence_output: [The output only lists the sub-section headings for 6.a-e and 8.a-b without the accompanying body text.]

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text that was successfully converted is accurate to the source. Minor differences like '8 points/m2' becoming '8 points/m²' are acceptable formatting normalizations.

checks.a_analysis: The output JSON was reviewed against the source PDF to identify any instances of duplicated content. No paragraphs or sections were found to be repeated in the output where they appear only once in the source.

The heading hierarchy in the JSON correctly follows a logical progression. The main committee title is H1, the 'MINUTES' title is H2, and major sections (6, 7, 8, 9) are correctly tagged as H2. While sections 1-5 are rendered as list items rather than headings, this is an acceptable structural choice and does not break navigation for screen readers as no levels are skipped (e.g., jumping from H1 to H3).
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, no structural corruption is possible.

The reading order and information flow are significantly compromised because the body text for several major sections is completely missing. Specifically, the detailed paragraphs describing the status reports for the Local Government Committee (6a), State Government Users Group Committee (6b), Federal Interagency Committee (6c), Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee (6d), Technical Advisory Committee (6e), LiDAR Data (8a), and Future M&O Meetings (8b) are absent. This omission breaks the logical flow of the minutes, as only headers remain for these items.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2, section 6.a: 'The LGC last met on November 20th... Accomplishments have been documented... Workshops will be scheduled...'. Page 3, section 6.e: 'Dan is stepping down from the role of TAC chair... Alex would like to see the TAC focus on public access...'. Page 3, section 8.a: 'Hope reported that Esri has offered to host dynamic web services...'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON output for elements 24, 26, and 30 contains the section labels within lists but is followed immediately by the next section header (e.g., element 27 '7. February 12, 2020 GICC Agenda') without the associated body paragraphs.

checks.a_analysis: The list structure is poorly preserved. First, the primary numbered list (1-9) in the source is fragmented: items 1-5 are individual unordered lists with missing numbering, while items 6-9 are converted to headings. Second, sub-items (a-e under section 6) are split across two different sections in the JSON (Section 2 and Section 3), breaking the continuity of the list. Third, the descriptive body text that belongs to each list item in sections 6 and 8 is missing entirely.
checks.b_evidence_source: 1. Welcome, 2. Approval of Minutes..., 3. NC Parcels Update..., 4. Census Update..., 5. Statewide Orthoimagery..., 6. Status Reports (with sub-items a-e), 7. February 12, 2020 GICC Agenda, 8. Other Items (with sub-items a-b), 9. Adjourn.
checks.b_evidence_output: Items 1-5 (RO 9, 11, 13, 15, 19) are separate single-item unordered lists. Sub-items 6a-c (RO 24) are in section 2, while sub-items 6d-e (RO 26) are in section 3.

checks.a_analysis: A hyperlink present in the source document is missing in the output. This occurred because the entire paragraph containing the link was omitted during the conversion process.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 3, Section 6.e: 'An item to work on is an update to the open source document to include...' (where 'document' is a blue hyperlink).
checks.b_evidence_output: The text for Section 6.e is entirely absent from the JSON structure.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully reproduces the visual organization of the source PDF. The document header information (Committee name, date, time, location) is correctly placed at the top. The numbered agenda items (1 through 9) from the PDF are rendered as structured headings and sections in the HTML. The hierarchy of sub-items under '6. Status Reports' is maintained. Although the HTML utilizes a single-column responsive layout and includes a navigation sidebar (Table of Contents), the visual flow and grouping of information match the source's intent.
The source PDF consists entirely of text, headings, and lists. There are no tables present in the document to evaluate.
The source PDF is a text-based minutes document and does not contain any meaningful images, logos, charts, or diagrams. Therefore, no images are expected in the HTML output.

There is no evidence of body content duplication. Each paragraph, list item, and section from the PDF appears exactly once in the main body of the HTML. The inclusion of section headings in the 'Contents' sidebar is an intentional accessibility and navigation feature and is not considered content duplication.
